pattern: Only the length of the current run of the target value matters, so a stream of unbounded length can be answered with a single counter that resets whenever the run breaks.
hints: You never need to remember the whole stream — only how many of the most recent values in a row have matched the target value.
Keep a running streak: increase it whenever the newest value equals the target, and reset it to zero the instant a different value arrives.
Each call answers true exactly when that streak has reached at least k; because the streak already accounts for every prior call, no lookback or stored history is required.

edge_inputs.description: k equals one, so any single matching call is already a run of length one
the target value never appears in the stream
the run reaches exactly k matches on the k-th call, then breaks on the next call
a single mismatch in the middle must fully reset the streak, not merely shrink it, before it can rebuild to k
several matching calls occur but never enough in a row to reach k, so every answer stays false
values sit at the top of the allowed range
the streak is broken and rebuilt twice, so only the two calls that complete a fresh run of k should read true

stress.description: a long randomized call sequence over a small value alphabet so streaks repeatedly build past k and break
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    value = rng.randint(1, 5)
    k = rng.randint(1, 6)
    ops = ['DataStream']
    args = [[value, k]]
    calls = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    for _ in range(calls):
        num = value if rng.random() < 0.5 else rng.randint(1, 5)
        ops.append('consec')
        args.append([num])
    return [ops, args]
